dataManager.title: Data Management
dataManager.description: Import and export your note data — your data belongs to you
dataManager.exportDescription: Export all notes as Markdown files organized by notebooks into a ZIP archive. Includes YAML frontmatter metadata, compatible with Obsidian, Typora, etc.
dataManager.importDescription: Import Markdown (.md), plain text (.txt), HTML (.html) files or ZIP archives. Compatible with Xiaomi Notes, OPPO/OnePlus Memo, vivo Notes export formats.
dataManager.dropFilesHere: Drop files here, or click to select
dataManager.supportedFiles: Supports .md, .txt, .html, .zip files (compatible with phone memo exports)
dataManager.dangerDescription: This will permanently delete all notes, tasks, tags, and notebooks, and reset the admin account. Export a backup first. This action cannot be undone!
dataManager.resetConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to erase all data?
dataManager.resetConfirmDesc: This will permanently erase all contents in the database, including:
dataManager.resetItem3: Admin password will be reset to admin123
dataManager.incorrectVerification: Please enter the correct verification word

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.addTaskPlaceholder: Add a new task, press Enter to save...

miCloud.title: Mi Cloud Import
miCloud.description: Import notes directly from Mi Cloud using your Cookie. No manual export needed. Supports batch selection, one-click import, and automatic image downloading.
miCloud.cookieRequired: Please enter your Mi Cloud Cookie
miCloud.cookieInvalid: Cookie is invalid or expired, please get a new one
miCloud.helpStep1: Open i.mi.com in your browser and sign in with your Xiaomi account
miCloud.helpStep2: Navigate to the Notes page after login
miCloud.helpStep3: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Network tab
miCloud.helpStep4: Refresh the page, click any request, find and copy the full Cookie from request headers
miCloud.cookieWarning: ⚠️ Cookie is equivalent to your account credentials, do not share with others. Cookie is only stored in the current session and will be cleared when the page is closed.

oppoCloud.title: OPPO Cloud Notes Import
oppoCloud.description: Import notes from OPPO (ColorOS) cloud sticky notes. Extract data from the OPPO cloud website via browser console, then paste here to import.
oppoCloud.helpStep1: Open cloud.heytap.com in your browser and sign in with your OPPO account
oppoCloud.helpStep2: Navigate to the Sticky Notes page
oppoCloud.helpStep3: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Console tab, paste the following script and press Enter:
oppoCloud.helpStep4: The script will try to extract notes automatically. If prompted to trigger data loading, click on the note categories on the left, then run window.__extractNotes() in the console
oppoCloud.helpStep5: After notes are copied to clipboard, come back to this page and paste into the input box below
oppoCloud.jsonPlaceholder: Paste the JSON data copied from the console here...
oppoCloud.jsonRequired: Please paste the notes JSON data
oppoCloud.invalidData: Invalid data format, please make sure you pasted the correct JSON data
oppoCloud.parseError: JSON parsing failed, please check the data format

iCloud.title: iPhone Notes Import
iCloud.description: Import notes from iPhone (iCloud) Notes. Supports extracting data via iCloud web or pasting JSON data directly.
iCloud.method1Step1: Open the Notes app on your Mac, select the notes you want to export
iCloud.method1Step2: Click menu "File → Export as PDF" or select text and copy/paste to .txt / .md files
iCloud.method1Step3: Use the "Import Notes" section above to import .txt / .md / .html files directly
iCloud.method2Title: Method 2: Extract via iCloud web console script
iCloud.method2Step1: Open icloud.com/notes in your browser and sign in with your Apple ID
iCloud.method2Step2: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Console tab
iCloud.method2Step3: Paste the following script and press Enter to run:
iCloud.method2Step4: Wait for the script to extract notes one by one (do not interact with the page)
iCloud.method2Step5: After notes are copied to clipboard, come back to this page and paste into the input box below
iCloud.tip: 💡 Method 1 is recommended for reliability. Method 2 depends on iCloud's DOM structure and may break if Apple updates the page.
iCloud.jsonPlaceholder: Paste the JSON data copied from the console here...
iCloud.jsonRequired: Please paste the notes JSON data
iCloud.invalidData: Invalid data format, please make sure you pasted the correct JSON data
iCloud.parseError: JSON parsing failed, please check the data format
